conditions.today: 6 AM-Well, well, well. We were certainly due for a snowstorm that overproduced on its promise, and yesterday's wild weather came through in the clutch. 9 inches of wind-driven pow plastered the upper slopes over the past 24 hours, with 3 of those sprinkled on after the lifts closed. The newly remodeled Rendezvous Mountain will meet Wednesday morning under mostly sunny skies that slowly transition to afternoon cloud cover, producing isolated snow showers. High temperatures will tickle the 20-degree mark outside the 10,400-foot Corbet's Cabin Waffle Factory and rise into the mid-30s down in Teton Village. Gusty winds from the west will be spinning the crystals you kick skyward as they crank in the 10-40 mph range. Who cares if it's the middle of the week? Succumb to the undeniable sunsation of getting lost in the glitterball flotation of a frosty face-shot's solar refraction. -Lege
conditions.yesterday: 6 AM is dawning, with ominous clouds fast encroaching on the Hole. But don't be alarmed; it's just an approaching round of snow, coming to re-stoke your soul. So, have no fear; grab your gear and come get weird. Scattered showers will be popping off through the morning in prelude to a steadier powfall this afternoon. Gusty west winds will be cranking in the 10 to 40 mph range, further cooling high temperatures that only reach the teens at the summit and the mid-30s in Teton Village. Everyone knows that eternal youth isn't really found in some kind of fountain. But the jury's still out on the effects of a snow-coated mountain. -Lege

trends: Great week for snow - 24" in last 7 days
recommendations: Fresh corduroy on Bivouac - challenging for advanced skiers
Fresh corduroy on St. John's - perfect for intermediate skiers
Fresh corduroy on Kemmerer (Lower) - perfect for intermediate skiers

morningBrief.headline: Fresh Tracks for Days
morningBrief.body: It's a 9" day. You know what to do. First chair or regret it. 8 runs freshly groomed, mostly in All Terrain.
